headline: Mental Health Services Reach Only 12% of Malaysians Who Need Them
context: National Health and Morbidity Survey 2025 estimates 4.2 million Malaysians need mental health support but only 500,000 access services.

cards.big: Only 12% of 4.2 million Malaysians needing mental health care receive any treatment
cards.sub: Treatment gap is worst in rural areas where services are virtually absent

cards.big: Malaysia has 410 psychiatrists for 33 million people or 1.2 per 100,000 population
cards.sub: WHO minimum recommendation is 3 psychiatrists per 100,000 population

cards.big: Mental Health Act 2001 has not been amended despite 24 years of changing practices
cards.sub: Outdated legislation does not support community-based mental health models

cards.big: Australia's Better Access program subsidized 8.4 million psychology sessions in 2024
cards.sub: Insurance-subsidized model dramatically increased treatment accessibility

cards.big: A mental health system that reaches 1 in 8 people in need is not a system—it is a gap
Should mental health consultations be covered under universal primary care benefits
